semesters.notes: Testing work–study–family balance with 1 course. Statistics is the right first course — directly required and well within background.
CIS 5150 (difficulty 3) + EAS 5240 (difficulty 2) = combined load of 5. Manageable with work schedule. Ethics is reading/writing — pairs well with a math course.
Accelerated ~10-week session. Strong algorithms background makes this feasible solo. Summer = fewer work distractions.
Systems programming. Single course to allow time to adapt to C system calls. C++ background makes this less daunting than it looks.
Machine Learning for DS. Sequenced after Statistics (ESE 5420) and Linear Algebra (CIS 5150) — the two foundations ML most depends on.
Databases. Weakest area from undergrad (CS 157A, B−). A focused summer term allows dedicated attention to SQL, relational theory, and system design.
Big Data Analytics. Extends SJSU CS 131 background at graduate level with heavier Spark / distributed systems engineering.
Technical Elective #1. CIS 5210 AI leverages strong SJSU CS 156 AI background (A+). Alternative: ESE 5460 Deep Learning.
Final open elective. Light finish to close out the degree. Course TBD based on career direction at the time. 🎓 Graduation: Fall 2029.
